title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: July 14, 2026: Mixed Market Sentiment as Inflation Data and Q2 Earnings Take Center Stage

items.headline: US Markets Navigate Geopolitical Tensions and Key Economic Releases
items.summary: US markets are navigating a mixed landscape on Tuesday, July 14, 2026, as investors weigh ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and surging oil prices against the commencement of Q2 earnings season and crucial inflation data. Major indices closed lower on Monday, with futures showing mixed movements early Tuesday.

items.headline: Vision Marine Technologies Jumps on Strong Q3 Results
items.summary: The company surged after reporting $48.6 million in revenue, $11.8 million in gross profit, and $2.4 million in cash from operating activities for the first nine months of fiscal 2026.

items.headline: Tower Semiconductor Surges on Expansion Plans
items.summary: Shares surged after the company unveiled a ~$3B investment to expand advanced semiconductor manufacturing capacity in Japan, supported by ~$1B in government grants, and raised its 2028 financial targets.

items.headline: Venture Global Gains After Equity Funding Round
items.summary: Venture Global jumped after closing an equity funding round for new LNG projects.

items.headline: IBM Plunges on Q2 Earnings Warning
items.summary: Shares are getting crushed after warning that preliminary second-quarter earnings fell short of expectations, blaming weakness in software and infrastructure.

items.headline: AppLovin Corporation Drops on Slowing Ad Growth
items.summary: The major loser of the S&P 500 Index, shares fell on concerns about slowing e-commerce advertising growth.

items.headline: Sandisk Declines Amid Chip Stock Pullback
items.summary: Declined as chip stocks pulled back following weakness in global memory names.

items.headline: Energy and Financials Lead, Tech and Semiconductors Lag
items.summary: On Monday, the Energy sector (XLE) rose 3.2%, with utilities and financials also showing strength. In contrast, Information Technology (XLK) fell 2.1%, Materials (XLB) declined 0.8%, and Communication Services (XLC) dropped 1%, largely driven by a significant pullback in semiconductor stocks.

items.headline: Fed Officials Signal Potential Rate Hikes Amid Persistent Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller indicated he is ready to support rate hikes in the 'near term' if inflation measures continue to rise, noting that core goods and services prices have been steadily increasing. Fed funds swaps imply roughly a 50% probability of a July rate hike.

items.headline: June CPI Report Released Today, Focus on Core Inflation
items.summary: The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics released the June Consumer Price Index (CPI) report today, July 14. Economists anticipate headline inflation to ease to around 3.8-3.92% year-over-year, but core inflation, excluding volatile food and energy, is expected to remain elevated at 2.8-2.85% annually.

items.headline: Oil Prices Surge Amid Escalating US-Iran Tensions
items.summary: Oil prices have surged, with West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude rising to around $80.80 per barrel and Brent crude to $87.23, due to escalating U.S.-Iran tensions and President Trump's announced measures concerning the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: Q2 Earnings Season Kicks Off with Major Bank Reports
items.summary: The second-quarter 2026 earnings season officially began today, with major Wall Street banks including JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Citigroup, Wells Fargo, and Goldman Sachs releasing their financial results. Goldman Sachs ($GS) reported strong Q2 performance with diluted EPS of $20.98.
